unknowns.reason: Required pointer, disabled-option, callback-order, dismissal, and focus vector is pending shared browser execution; no pass is claimed.
Full Arrow/Home/End/Page/Escape/typeahead/scroll behavior is required but pending shared browser execution.
Controlled ownership, Tab closure, and exact callback ordering are pending shared browser execution.
Object identity and multiple selection require executable browser fixtures and are not inferred from declarations.
proof/select/receipts.json records React and Vue blocked before meaningful interaction because harness queries returned null; their interaction gates remain blocked.
Existing receipt records failed pointer open/select, typeahead, controlled value/open, and callback ordering. These failures remain required parity gates.
Existing receipt records failed pointer open/select, typeahead, controlled state, callback ordering, hydration, and focus/scroll. These failures remain visible and unresolved.
No focused canonical @cloudflare/kumo@2.5.2 browser execution establishes exact event order, focus/scroll, outside dismissal, typeahead timeout/normalization, or object comparator behavior.

Anchor-width minimum, available-height scrolling, collision behavior, provider/custom/body portal precedence, open SSR, portal relocation, generated IDs, warning-free hydration, and node preservation require direct evidence.
Required Chromium, Firefox, WebKit, RTL, zoom, reduced motion, mobile/coarse pointer, stylesheet presence, icon loading, error/loading/Field variants, and animation reversal vectors have not run.
Tooltip timing and arbitrary icon component rendering are delegated to Tooltip and React and are not guessed by this contract.
Declarations guarantee outside-click resistance but do not state Escape policy; delegated browser evidence was not available for an exact claim.
Title/description association ids are generated by Base UI and literal values are not stable contract surface.
Open content is portaled and the shared runner's root selection convention may require adapting the alertdialog vector without changing canonical semantics.
Portal positioning, outside interaction, dismissal ordering, ARIA association, and focus restoration require the pending shared browser adapter.
Controlled open callback semantics are a required browser vector and are not fabricated from SSR.
Collision fallback order, arrow placement, available-size variables, RTL, zoom, viewport edges, virtual anchors, and fixed versus absolute coordinates need direct browser observation.
Exact outside pointer/focus phase, preventDefault behavior, nested layers, initial-focus policy, Tab behavior, and restoration ordering are delegated to Base UI.
Custom/provider/body container precedence is declared; SSR open markup, portal relocation, generated ID stability, warnings, and node preservation remain unexecuted.
Callback detail object and browser/platform edge behavior are inherited from Base UI and are intentionally not guessed.
Reset/autofill and platform activation details inherited from Base UI are outside the canonical Kumo declaration and are not guessed.
Base UI event details, autofill/password managers, IME, RTL, selection ranges, clipboard rejection, and platform accessibility-tree differences remain inherited/vendor unknowns.

Whether a given label set overflows and exact scrollLeft changes depend on measured browser layout, fonts, and viewport; no browser pass is claimed.
Base UI may emit generated identifiers and additional state attributes; their exact values are implementation-managed and are intentionally not frozen.
Canonical Tabs 2.5.2 has no panel API and renders no tabpanel; panel lifecycle, aria-controls linkage to consumer content, and content visibility must not be invented.
Options require ReactNode icons and function callbacks not representable by the serializable fixture DSL; shared browser fixture execution is required.
ID matching and arrow wrap vector is authored as a requirement but pending shared browser execution.
Tooltip portal DOM, accessible description relationship, delays, hover/focus dismissal, and hydration are outside the MenuBar chunk and unobserved.
Listener behavior during focus movement, dynamic option changes, disabled descendants, and focus-in/out ordering require browser execution.
MenuBar has no responsive, portal, positioning, selection typeahead, or dismissal API of its own; only nested Tooltip may portal/dismiss.

SSR always evaluates isMobile=false, while the first client render reads matchMedia synchronously. A narrow viewport can therefore choose desktop aside on the server and mobile portal/nav on hydration; no identical-hydration or browser-pass claim is made.

Mobile, CollapsibleContent, and SlidingView apply/remove inert imperatively in ref callbacks. aria-hidden is SSR-observable, but inert presence before/through hydration must be browser-observed and is not asserted action-free.
Exact portal placement, focus restoration ordering, and focusout interactions require a browser; behavior is documented from canonical runtime but no browser pass is claimed.
CollapsibleTrigger requires a render ReactElement. The generic JSON composition DSL cannot encode a ReactElement-valued prop, so trigger linkage is not fabricated in the action-free vector.
The public direction prop is declared and defaults left, but canonical runtime computes translation solely from active child index; no direction-dependent observable effect is invented.
Item render clones a consumer element with anchor-like props; its resulting tag, router behavior, and focus semantics depend on that supplied element and are not invented.
Canonical TableOfContents performs no observation or scroll tracking; which item is active and whether multiple active flags are supplied is wholly consumer-controlled.
The caller-provided React element controls the root.
The caller-provided React element controls the polymorphic root.
The render prop or LinkProvider controls the framework-specific root.
RTL wrapping, Home/End, and browser-specific roving-focus edge behavior inherited from Base UI are not declared by Kumo and are not guessed.
Autocomplete.Content uses Base UI Portal without exposing a container prop; exact portal target under provider/shadow DOM was not asserted.
Closed action-free SSR is supported; open portal markup and generated ids depend on Base UI and were not frozen as exact strings.
Base UI generates input/listbox ids; relationships are required but literal ids are intentionally not frozen.
Exact nested chip/input DOM depends on renderItem and controlled value; behavior is specified without inventing a universal fixture shape.
A controlled closed portal may yield no rendered element; shared runner needs a mount-host convention before root expectations can distinguish host from component output.
Canonical wrapper documents selection but Item click ownership remains consumer-provided; exact event log is not asserted.
Action-free closed SSR is supported; exact open portal output and generated ids are Base UI implementation details.
Calendar strings, week layout, and locale-specific formatting are owned by react-day-picker and Intl/browser locale; this contract intentionally does not invent locale behavior.
The component initializes from the current local month and generates labels with browser locale APIs; exact dates and strings are deliberately not frozen or invented.
Required browser vector is authored but pending the shared browser adapter; event order and close-on-select are not claimed as passing.
Required keyboard/typeahead/submenu vector is pending shared browser execution.
Exact outside-event phase, cancellation, link activation, close-on-select defaults, callback order, and focus restoration are delegated to Base UI and not directly observed.
Collision fallback, RTL, nested-layer arbitration, custom container behavior, portal relocation, deterministic IDs, and hydration node preservation need browser evidence.
Matching normalization and timeout are Base UI behavior and were not directly observed.
Assistive technology announcement timing and animation-completion removal timing are inherited from Base UI, CSS, and the user agent; exact wall-clock timing is not guessed.
Dropdown popup geometry, native accessibility projection, and keyboard details are inherited from the Kumo Select/Base UI implementation and browser; they are not guessed here.
